strengths: Coverage is effectively complete at 100% with no pending files.
The pack captures 5 canonical flows, which gives downstream models a usable execution map.
Change playbooks and impact guidance are present, which makes the pack useful for execution as well as understanding.

probes.prompt: Read-only repo briefing for torque-ai. Return exactly 4 bullets and keep it under 140 words. 1. Primary runtime entrypoint. 2. Secondary important entrypoint or startup surface. 3. One high-risk subsystem or hotspot worth understanding first. 4. One validation command worth running first.

probes.prompt: What invariant governs Task lifecycle in torque-ai, and where should an engineer look before editing it?
readiness_signals: Submission surfaces should delegate into handlers and the task manager instead of invoking providers directly.
Task requests enter through API or handlers, start in the task manager, route through execution modules, and finish in the completion pipeline.

probes.prompt: What invariant governs Task lifecycle in torque-ai, and where should an engineer look before editing it?
readiness_signals: Task completion should converge through the finalizer and completion pipeline so verification and follow-up hooks stay centralized.
Task requests enter through API or handlers, start in the task manager, route through execution modules, and finish in the completion pipeline.

probes.prompt: If you change Editing Control-plane API in torque-ai, which files should you edit, which tests should you run, and which invariants must you recheck?
readiness_signals: npx vitest run server/tests/api-server-core.test.js server/tests/v2-governance-handlers.test.js server/tests/claude-event-hooks.test.js server/tests/api-middleware.test.js
Routes and control-plane transports should validate and normalize requests, then delegate into handlers or execution services.
Business rules should stay in handler or execution layers so API surfaces remain thin and consistent across transports.
Schedules should create tracked task or tool executions; background automation should not fire invisibly.

probes.prompt: If you change Editing Task execution pipeline in torque-ai, which files should you edit, which tests should you run, and which invariants must you recheck?
readiness_signals: npx vitest run server/tests/bug-001-override-provider.test.js server/tests/fallback-retry.test.js server/tests/host-distribution.test.js server/tests/process-lifecycle.test.js
Routing decisions should be derived from provider health, capabilities, and policy-aware scoring rather than one-off special cases.
Retry and fallback should preserve task ownership and emit observable state transitions.
Submission surfaces should delegate into handlers and the task manager instead of invoking providers directly.

probes.prompt: If you change Editing Workflow orchestration in torque-ai, which files should you edit, which tests should you run, and which invariants must you recheck?
readiness_signals: Workflow structure, runtime unblocking, and await reporting should stay aligned on the same DAG semantics.
Dependent nodes should unblock only from persisted workflow state, not from ad hoc in-memory assumptions.

probes.prompt: Explain how Validation and tests connects to Persistence and scheduling in torque-ai. Where is the boundary, and which files show the integration seam?
readiness_signals: Validation and tests depends on Persistence and scheduling.

probes.prompt: If you edit server/tests/vitest-setup.js in torque-ai, which nearby files and tests should you inspect first, and why is that file a risky seam?
readiness_signals: High fan-in (240) and fan-out (8) inside Validation and tests.
